actionUnits.condition: Fire component (MonoBehaviour, script GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) configured as in scene meta: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance overridden by PrefabInstance to 3.5, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5. This step waits for automatic ignition when >=2 neighbor Fire instances within maxNeighborDistance are on. Expected behavior: after waiting up to timeToOn, Fire.UpdateFireState(true) is invoked internally causing ParticleSystem to Play and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). Note: No UnityEvent m_Calls are present to invoke manually.

Manual test condition to validate water extinguish logic on Fire (GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). External water system should repeatedly call IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) on this object with hit intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) until accumulated currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). Expected: Fire turns off, UpdateFireState(true) is executed internally to Stop ParticleSystem and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Since WaterHit has parameters and there is no MonoBehaviour parameterless public method or UnityEvent m_Calls exposed, this must be driven by another object (e.g., a hose/projectile) colliding and invoking WaterHit.
